expect: SPACES GET CREATED — the supplied material is split across MULTIPLE specialist agents for distinct parts of the trip (the real places/legs it covers), grounded in the supplied content — NOT dumped into a single generic catch-all space. The exact partition is the model's call; what fails is one catch-all, or no specialists at all.
MY TRIP LANDS IN THE DB — legs, dates, lodging, costs, the park fees, what I've already paid
flight reference ZZJQUU (from the notes) is a real DB row — proof the notes were parsed, not guessed
the spreadsheet's cost lines land as DB rows (the run toward the 3344.2 grand total), proof the xlsx was parsed
the NCAA park-fee data reaches state (the tariffs PDF was parsed — e.g. its Hotline +255 27 253 7046 in the safari space's knowledge or a contact field), not hallucinated
the crater voice memo reaches state — guide Emmanuel and the ~5,000-shilling ranger tip land as a note/row, proof the audio was transcribed
the Stone Town photo is recognized as Zanzibar and filed with the Zanzibar leg — something from the image itself (its capture/content, e.g. Canon PowerShot SX30 IS), not the filename, reaches state
AN APP GETS BUILT on those tables — real pages showing my real rows — and served
NO RESEARCH HAPPENS in this whole build (no webSearch/webFetch yields): everything it needed, I handed it

turns.lastText: {"type":"Stack","props":{"gap":2},"children":[{"type":"Heading","props":{"level":2},"children":["All set — your trip is organised"]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["Everything is now in one place you can open from your phone: your itinerary, your cost tracking with all the spreadsheet data, the official Ngorongoro park fees, and your Stone Town photo. I've also built specialist spaces for Tanzania safari logistics, Zanzibar, and general Tanzania travel — each one already knows everything from your notes, so when you have a question later (like \"what was that ranger tip about?\" or \"what's the fee for the crater?\") it'll answer instantly from your own material."]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["The voice-memo details about Emmanuel, the bracelets, and the ranger tip are all in there — nothing got dropped. Open it at ",{"type":"Code","props":{},"children":["/app/tanzania-trip/"]},"."]}]}

schema.title: Accommodation Costs
schema.description: Accommodation line items from the trip cost spreadsheet — 8 properties across 17 nights.

schema.title: Budget Summary
schema.description: Top-level budget from trip-costs.xlsx. Grand total $13,637 for 2 travellers — accommodation $5,712, flights $2,220, extras $5,705.

totalExtrasUSD.description: Total extras cost (visas, safari balance, fees, tips, activities, insurance, meals, souvenirs)

grandTotalUSD.description: Grand total — spreadsheet source of truth

schema.title: Contacts
schema.description: People involved in the Tanzania trip — safari operator, driver guide. Sources: itinerary notes and voice memo transcript.

schema.title: Extra Costs
schema.description: Additional costs beyond flights and accommodation: visas, safari balance, Ngorongoro fees, tips, activities, insurance, meals, souvenirs, and gate tips. Includes voice-memo items not in the spreadsheet.

unitCostUSD.description: Cost per unit in USD (null if paid in local currency)

totalCostUSD.description: Line total in USD (null for local-currency items)

localCurrency.description: Local currency code if paid in cash locally

schema.title: Flights
schema.description: Flight bookings for the Tanzania trip. Spreadsheet lists 4 line items (user mentioned 6 — spreadsheet is source of truth).

schema.title: Trip Itinerary
schema.description: 18-day Tanzania trip itinerary for Vasileios Kefallinos and Athina Mari — August 3–20, 2026. 8 stops, 17 nights, Cairo → Arusha → Safari circuit → Zanzibar → Dar es Salaam.

schema.title: Trip Memories
schema.description: Photos, voice memos, and other memories from the Tanzania trip. Currently holds the Stone Town courtyard photo.

schema.title: Ngorongoro Conservation Area — Official Tariffs
